page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i4.large is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, with a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0. It includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

For storage-intensive workloads, the instance is equipped with 479 GB of local SSD storage. It operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The hardware profile offers a balanced qualitative cost efficiency by bundling dedicated Intel Xeon processing cores with high-speed local solid-state storage and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, reducing the need for external storage attachments for localized data operations.

Given the combination of dedicated CPU cores, a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and integrated local SSD storage, this instance is suited for database hosting, caching layers, and localized data processing tasks that require low-latency storage access and moderate memory capacity without the overhead of GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i4.large is an x86_64 server instance running on the KVM hypervisor, powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, offering a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 479 GB of local SSD storage. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps and no GPU acceleration, this configuration provides a balanced resource profile. The combination of dedicated processing, a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, and built-in local SSD storage makes this instance qualitatively cost-effective for localized data operations. It is well-suited for database workloads, caching systems, and general-purpose applications requiring low-latency local storage.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core x86_64 server featuring integrated local SSD storage and a high memory-to-CPU ratio for localized database workloads.
